starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg package

class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.AdaParameter(alpha: float = 0.5, min_alpha: float = 0.1, max_alpha: float = 0.95)[source]

Bases: object

Adaptive threshold for HistoricalCache — adjusts based on sync/compute latency ratio.

alpha: float = 0.5
max_alpha: float = 0.95
min_alpha: float = 0.1
update_from_latency(sync_ms: float, compute_ms: float) → None[source]

Tighten threshold when sync is cheap relative to compute; loosen otherwise.

class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.AsyncExchangeHandle(handle: Any, output_buffer: Tensor, recv_counts: list[int], slot_width: int, value_dim: int, device: str)[source]

Bases: object

Handle for a non-blocking all_to_all_single exchange.

device: str
handle: Any
output_buffer: Tensor
recv_counts: list[int]
slot_width: int
value_dim: int
wait() → tuple[Tensor, Tensor][source]
class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.CTDGDataBatch(index: int, split: str, event_ids: Tensor, src: Tensor, dst: Tensor, ts: Tensor, edge_feat: Tensor)[source]

Bases: object

A mini-batch of continuous-time temporal events.

Attributes:

index: Batch index within the current epoch. split: "train", "val", or "test". event_ids: Global event indices, shape [B]. src: Source node IDs, shape [B]. dst: Destination node IDs, shape [B]. ts: Timestamps, shape [B]. edge_feat: Edge feature matrix, shape [B, E].

dst: Tensor
edge_feat: Tensor
event_ids: Tensor
index: int
property is_empty: bool
property size: int
split: str
src: Tensor
ts: Tensor
class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.CTDGFeatureRoute(route_type: 'str', world_size: 'int', replicated_memory: 'bool' = True)[source]

Bases: object

describe() → dict[str, Any][source]
exchange(ctx: DistributedContext, node_ids: torch.Tensor, values: torch.Tensor, async_op: bool = False) → tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor] | AsyncExchangeHandle[source]

Exchange node features with all peers via all-to-all.

Args:

ctx: DistributedContext with rank / world_size. node_ids: Global node IDs to exchange, shape [N]. values: Feature values to exchange, shape [N, D]. async_op: If True, return an AsyncExchangeHandle instead of blocking.

Returns:

If async_op is False: (merged_ids, merged_values) with features from all ranks concatenated. If True: an AsyncExchangeHandle to call .wait() on later.

replicated_memory: bool = True
route_type: str
world_size: int
class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.CTDGHistoricalCache(num_shared: int, hidden_dim: int, device: str, num_nodes: int | None = None, shared_node_ids: torch.Tensor | None = None, ada_param: AdaParameter | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Per-rank cache for shared-node memory snapshots.

Tracks the last-synced memory for shared nodes and provides a boolean mask indicating which nodes have changed enough to warrant a new sync.

bind_shared_nodes(num_nodes: int, shared_node_ids: Tensor) → None[source]

Bind fixed global shared-node IDs to cache slot indices.

historical_check(shared_node_local_indices: Tensor, current_memory: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Check which shared nodes have drifted beyond the sync threshold.

Computes cosine distance between current and cached memory and returns True for nodes that exceed (1 - alpha).

Args:

shared_node_local_indices: Local node IDs of shared nodes, [S]. current_memory: Current memory vectors, [S, D].

Returns:

Boolean mask [S], True for nodes needing sync.

slot_indices(node_ids: Tensor) → Tensor[source]
synchronize_shared_update(received_node_ids: Tensor, received_values: Tensor, received_ts: Tensor) → None[source]

Apply received updates to cached snapshot, keeping only newer timestamps.

update_cache(shared_indices: Tensor, new_memory: Tensor, new_ts: Tensor) → None[source]

Update cache after a successful sync.

class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.CTDGLinkPredictor(num_nodes: int, hidden_dim: int, edge_feat_dim: int, dim_time: int = 100, num_head: int = 2, dropout: float = 0.1, att_dropout: float = 0.1)[source]

Bases: Module

Temporal link prediction module with transformer attention.

Combines a TemporalTransformerConv for computing node representations from sampled neighborhoods, with an edge predictor that scores (src, dst) and (src, neg_dst) pairs.

Args:

num_nodes: Total number of nodes in the graph. hidden_dim: Node memory / representation dimension. edge_feat_dim: Edge feature dimension. dim_time: Time encoding dimension (default 100). num_head: Number of attention heads (default 2). dropout: Dropout rate for output projection. att_dropout: Dropout rate for attention weights.

Forward signature:

output = predictor(src_conv, dst_conv, neg_conv)
# Returns CTDGModelOutput with .pos_logits and .neg_logits
forward(src_conv: Tensor, dst_conv: Tensor, neg_conv: Tensor) → CTDGModelOutput[source]

Defines the computation performed at every call.

Should be overridden by all subclasses.

Note

Although the recipe for forward pass needs to be defined within this function, one should call the Module instance afterwards instead of this since the former takes care of running the registered hooks while the latter silently ignores them.

training: bool
class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.CTDGMemoryBank(num_nodes: int, hidden_dim: int, device: str, mailbox_slots: int = 1, edge_feat_dim: int = 0, rank: int = 0, world_size: int = 1, async_sync: bool = True, node_parts: torch.Tensor | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Per-node memory + K-slot mailbox.

In distributed mode each rank only allocates storage for its own partition (num_local_nodes nodes). Global node IDs are mapped to local IDs via global2local. Nodes not owned by this rank use a temporary CPU buffer (remote_memory) that is filled by async sync operations.

In single-rank mode num_local_nodes == num_nodes and the mapping is the identity, so all existing call sites work unchanged.

async_sync: bool = True
describe() → dict[str, Any][source]
device: str
edge_feat_dim: int = 0
gather(node_ids: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Read memory vectors for the given global node IDs.

Args:

node_ids: 1-D tensor of global node IDs (any device).

Returns:

Tensor [len(node_ids), hidden_dim] on self.device. Nodes not owned by this rank get zero vectors.

gather_mailbox(node_ids: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Legacy single-slot gather (slot 0).

hidden_dim: int
property last_update: Tensor
mailbox_slots: int = 1
node_parts: torch.Tensor | None = None
num_nodes: int
rank: int = 0
read_mailbox(node_ids: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Read K-slot mailbox for the given global node IDs.

Args:

node_ids: 1-D tensor of global node IDs.

Returns:

Tensor [len(node_ids), K, slot_width] on self.device.

read_mailbox_ts(node_ids: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Return mailbox timestamps [M, K] on self.device.

submit_async_mail_sync(ctx: DistributedContext, node_ids: Tensor, mail_slots: Tensor, mail_ts: Tensor) → None[source]

No-op for single rank; distributed path mirrors memory sync.

submit_async_memory_sync(ctx: DistributedContext, node_ids: Tensor, values: Tensor, timestamps: Tensor) → None[source]

Submit an async distributed memory sync for remote nodes.

Packs updated memory for nodes owned by other ranks and sends via all_to_all_single. In single-rank mode, falls back to a local memory update. Uses CTDGHistoricalCache (if available) to skip syncs for nodes that haven’t changed significantly.

Args:

ctx: DistributedContext with rank / world_size. node_ids: Global node IDs with updated memory, shape [N]. values: Updated memory vectors (detached), shape [N, D]. timestamps: Update timestamps, shape [N].

sync_updates(ctx, node_ids, values, timestamps)[source]
wait_pending_syncs() → None[source]

Block until all pending async memory and mailbox syncs complete.

Drains _pending_memory_syncs and _pending_mail_syncs, applies received updates to local storage, and updates the historical cache if present.

world_size: int = 1
write_mailbox(src_ids: Tensor, dst_ids: Tensor, src_mem: Tensor, dst_mem: Tensor, edge_feat: Tensor, timestamps: Tensor) → None[source]

Write new messages into the mailbox for src and dst nodes.

Each message slot contains [src_mem || dst_mem || edge_feat] concatenated. Messages are written in timestamp order using a circular buffer (next_mail_pos).

Args:

src_ids: Source node global IDs, shape [B]. dst_ids: Destination node global IDs, shape [B]. src_mem: Source memory before update, shape [B, D]. dst_mem: Destination memory before update, shape [B, D]. edge_feat: Edge features, shape [B, E]. timestamps: Event timestamps, shape [B].

class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.CTDGMemoryUpdater(hidden_dim: int, mailbox_slot_dim: int, mailbox_slots: int)[source]

Bases: Module

GRU-based memory updater over K-slot mailbox history.

Aggregates K mailbox slots via a GRU (mail_aggregator), then fuses the aggregated message with the current node memory via a GRU cell.

Args:

hidden_dim: Node memory dimension. mailbox_slot_dim: Width of each mailbox slot (2*D + E). mailbox_slots: Number of mailbox slots (K).

Forward signature:

new_memory = updater(mailbox_history, current_memory)
# mailbox_history: [M, K, slot_dim]
# current_memory:  [M, D]
# returns:         [M, D]
forward(mailbox_history: Tensor, current_memory: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Defines the computation performed at every call.

Should be overridden by all subclasses.

Note

Although the recipe for forward pass needs to be defined within this function, one should call the Module instance afterwards instead of this since the former takes care of running the registered hooks while the latter silently ignores them.

training: bool
class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.CTDGModelOutput(pos_logits: 'torch.Tensor', neg_logits: 'torch.Tensor')[source]

Bases: object

neg_logits: Tensor
pos_logits: Tensor
class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.CTDGOnlineRuntime(dataset: TGTemporalDataset, sampler: NativeTemporalSampler, memory: CTDGMemoryBank, model: CTDGLinkPredictor, optimizer: torch.optim.Optimizer, route: CTDGFeatureRoute, device: str, dist_ctx: DistributedContext, memory_updater: CTDGMemoryUpdater | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

End-to-end CTDG online runtime for temporal link prediction.

Orchestrates the full pipeline per batch:

  1. Negative sampling — sample negatives from the dst node pool.

  2. BTS temporal neighbor sampling — via NativeTemporalSampler.

  3. Memory read & update — read old memory, run CTDGMemoryUpdater, write updated memory back to CTDGMemoryBank.

  4. Mailbox write — store [src_mem, dst_mem, edge_feat] slots.

  5. Temporal attention — CTDGLinkPredictor conv over sampled DGL block with memory as node features.

  6. Loss & metrics — BPR loss + AP / AUC / MRR.

  7. Async distributed sync — submit memory/mail syncs for remote nodes.

Args:

dataset: TGTemporalDataset for batching. sampler: NativeTemporalSampler for neighbor sampling. memory: CTDGMemoryBank for per-node state. model: CTDGLinkPredictor (possibly DDP-wrapped). optimizer: PyTorch optimizer. route: CTDGFeatureRoute for distributed exchange. device: Target device string. dist_ctx: DistributedContext. memory_updater: Optional CTDGMemoryUpdater.

Example:

runtime = CTDGOnlineRuntime(...)
for batch in runtime.iter_batches("train", batch_size=200):
    result = runtime.train_step(batch)
    print(result["loss"], result["meta"]["metrics"]["ap"])
eval_step(batch: CTDGDataBatch, split: str) → dict[str, Any][source]

Execute one evaluation step (no-grad, with memory update).

Memory and mailbox are still updated during eval to maintain temporal consistency (mirrors the MemShare validation loop).

Args:

batch: A CTDGDataBatch mini-batch. split: "val" or "test".

Returns:

Dict with "loss", "predictions", "targets", "meta".

iter_batches(split: str, batch_size: int) → Iterable[CTDGDataBatch][source]
predict_step(batch: CTDGDataBatch) → dict[str, Any][source]
train_step(batch: CTDGDataBatch) → dict[str, Any][source]

Execute one training step.

Steps: drain pending syncs -> negative sample -> BTS sample -> memory update -> mailbox write -> conv forward -> loss + backward -> optimizer step -> submit async syncs.

Args:

batch: A CTDGDataBatch mini-batch.

Returns:

Dict with "loss" (float), "predictions" (list), "targets" (list), "meta" (dict with metrics, timings).

class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.CTDGPreprocessor[source]

Bases: GraphPreprocessor

build_partitions(session_ctx: SessionContext) → None[source]
build_runtime_artifacts(session_ctx: SessionContext) → PreparedArtifacts[source]
graph_mode = 'ctdg'
prepare_raw(session_ctx: SessionContext) → None[source]
class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.CTDGSampleOutput(split: 'str', root_nodes: 'torch.Tensor', timestamps: 'torch.Tensor', sampled_nodes: 'torch.Tensor', sampled_eids: 'torch.Tensor', blocks: 'list[object]')[source]

describe() → dict[str, int][source]

sampled_eids: Tensor
sampled_nodes: Tensor
split: str
timestamps: Tensor
class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.CTDGSession[source]

Bases: object

Thin CTDG session replacing the old CTDGProvider.

Usage:

session = CTDGSession()
session.prepare_data(ctx)
session.build_runtime(ctx)
for batch in session.iter_train(ctx):
    result = session.train_step(batch)
session.save_checkpoint("ckpt.pt")
build_runtime(session_ctx: SessionContext) → None[source]
eval_step(batch: Any) → dict[str, Any][source]
iter_eval(session_ctx: SessionContext, split: str = 'val')[source]
iter_predict(session_ctx: SessionContext, split: str = 'test')[source]
iter_train(session_ctx: SessionContext)[source]
load_checkpoint(path: str | Path) → None[source]
predict_step(batch: Any) → dict[str, Any][source]
prepare_data(session_ctx: SessionContext) → PreparedArtifacts[source]
save_checkpoint(path: str | Path) → None[source]
train_step(batch: Any) → dict[str, Any][source]
class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.NativeTemporalSampler(dataset: TGTemporalDataset, fanout: list[int], history: int, strategy: str, workers: int = 1)[source]

Bases: object

Multi-hop temporal neighbor sampler backed by the C++ BTS library.

Lazily builds a per-split BTS sampler on first call and caches it.

Args:

dataset: TGTemporalDataset providing the event graph. fanout: Per-layer fanout list (e.g., [10, 10] for 2-hop with 10 neighbors each). history: Number of sampler layers (typically len(fanout)). strategy: Sampling strategy string (e.g., "recent"). workers: Number of BTS sampler worker threads.

Example:

sampler = NativeTemporalSampler(dataset, fanout=[10, 10],
                                history=2, strategy="recent")
output = sampler.sample("train", root_nodes, timestamps)
sample(split: str, root_nodes: Tensor, timestamps: Tensor) → CTDGSampleOutput[source]

Sample temporal neighbors for the given root nodes.

Args:

split: "train", "val", or "test" — determines which temporal graph to sample from. root_nodes: Root node IDs to sample around, shape [R]. timestamps: Timestamps for each root node, shape [R].

Returns:

CTDGSampleOutput with sampled nodes, edges, and BTS blocks.

Raises:

RuntimeError: If the native BTS sampler is not available.

class starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.TGTemporalDataset(root: str | Path, name: str, split_ratio: dict[str, float] | None = None, config: dict | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Temporal graph dataset with train/val/test splitting.

Loads raw temporal events via load_raw_temporal_events(), stores them as flat tensors (src, dst, ts, edge_feat), and provides batched iteration with distributed sharding.

Args:

root: Root directory containing dataset folders. name: Dataset name (subdirectory under root). split_ratio: Optional custom split ratio dict. config: Full session config dict.

Example:

dataset = TGTemporalDataset("/data/raw", "wikipedia")
for batch in dataset.iter_batches("train", batch_size=200):
    print(batch.src.shape)
configure_partition(*, node_parts: torch.Tensor | None, edge_parts: torch.Tensor | None, rank: int, world_size: int) → None[source]
describe() → dict[str, int][source]
iter_batches(split: str, batch_size: int) → Iterator[CTDGDataBatch][source]

Iterate over mini-batches for the given split.

Args:

split: "train", "val", or "test". batch_size: Number of events per batch.

Yields:

CTDGDataBatch instances.

sampler_graph(split: str) → dict[str, Tensor][source]

Build the temporal neighbor graph for the BTS sampler.

Returns a dict with "row", "col", "eid", "ts" tensors covering all events visible to the given split (train events for "train", train+val for "val", all for "test").

Args:

split: "train", "val", or "test".

Returns:

Dict of CPU tensors suitable for build_temporal_neighbor_block().

split_event_ids(split: str) → Tensor[source]
starry_unigraph.backends.ctdg.build_ctdg_model(model_family: str, *, num_nodes: int, hidden_dim: int, edge_feat_dim: int, dim_time: int = 100, num_head: int = 2, dropout: float = 0.1, att_dropout: float = 0.1) → Module[source]

Instantiate a CTDG model by family.

Args:

model_family: CTDG model family name from the config. num_nodes: Total number of nodes in the graph. hidden_dim: Node memory / representation dimension. edge_feat_dim: Edge feature dimension. dim_time: Time encoding dimension. num_head: Number of attention heads. dropout: Dropout rate for output projection. att_dropout: Dropout rate for attention weights.

Returns:

A CTDG model instance.

Raises:

KeyError: If the family is not registered.

Note

This package is a backward-compatibility shim that re-exports classes from starry_unigraph.runtime.online. See the runtime/online subpackage for the canonical implementations.
